summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a scorching ’91 show highlighted by an epic Jack Straw and fiery China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider sequence.
review: The recording is a high‑fidelity soundboard source that many fans describe as crisp, clear and “crystal‑clear,” making every jam shine.  Musically the concert is praised for its tight first set, a blistering Jack Straw, a crowd‑pleasing China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, and soaring moments on Estimated, Stella Blue and Cassidy, with Jerry’s guitar and Bruce/Vince’s keys in top form.  A few reviewers note a missing intro to the China Cat jam and one off‑key vocal from Bruce, but overall the performance is regarded as one of the year’s standouts.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars, repeatedly praising both the musical performance and the soundboard quality; only minor criticisms appear, supporting a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners call the board “crystal‑clear,” “very clear,” and note excellent instrument separation. A few mention an older DAT source with limited high‑end, but the dominant consensus is that this version is clean and well‑mixed.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
poor_songs: China Cat Sunflower (intro missing in some copies)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as tight and “smoking,” building into a high‑energy Jack Straw that launches the show.  The second set opens with a crowded China Cat → Rider, followed by deep grooves on Estimated and soaring jams on Stella Blue and Cassidy, keeping the momentum relentless.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for blistering guitar tone, “smokes” on Jack Straw and other jams; one reviewer notes he sounds like he’s about to jump out of his jeans.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for powerful bass lines (“bass bombs”), vocal work on Box of Rain, and energetic stage presence (dancing up and down the fretboard).
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for trading verses with Jerry in the first set and delivering strong vocals; occasional reference to “Bob and Jer going back‑and‑forth.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby’s piano/keys are called “pumping,” “pushing limits,” and often “in the mix well throughout”; Vince is mentioned as part of the keyboard team, with both receiving high praise.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s time‑warp drumming is highlighted as a show highlight; overall drum work described as “great” and “the best.”
